race.name: Haldo Hansson Minne
race.tagline: A memorial race in Åsarna honoring a legendary Jämtland skier — forty-two kilometers of Swedish mountain tradition.

vitals.registration: Online registration via Swedish Ski Federation
vitals.aid_stations: 3 stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: Jämtland's inland climate produces cold, stable winter conditions. Temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C in late January. Reliable natural snow cover. The inland position shields from Atlantic storms but produces consistently cold racing.
challenges: Late January cold — often below -15°C at start
Short daylight hours in northern Sweden

course.primary: Loop course through the Åsarna forest and mountain terrain in central Jämtland. Rolling terrain with moderate climbing, groomed through mixed birch and spruce forest. A classic Swedish ski marathon profile.
course.surface: Groomed classic track through forest and mountain terrain
course.grooming: Well-groomed by Åsarna IF, standard Swedish marathon quality
features: Historic race honoring Haldo Hansson, legendary Jämtland skier
Central Jämtland mountain and forest terrain
One of Sweden's oldest ski marathons

history.summary: Founded in 1946 as a memorial race for Haldo Hansson (1891-1944), a legendary skier from Åsarna in Jämtland who was one of Sweden's finest cross-country competitors in the early 20th century. The race has been organized by Åsarna IF continuously and remains one of Sweden's most historically significant ski marathons, deeply rooted in the Jämtland skiing tradition.
notable_facts: Haldo Hansson was a dominant Swedish skier in the 1910s and 1920s
One of Sweden's oldest continuously held ski marathons
Åsarna IF, the organizing club, was founded in 1908
Jämtland has produced many of Sweden's greatest cross-country skiers

youtube_data.search_query: Haldo Hansson Minne ski race Åsarna Jämtland
